steps.title: Factor out the GCF and identify where the factor comes from
steps.content: The expression $2x^3 + 42x^2 + 208x$ has a greatest common factor of $2x$. Factoring it out:$$2x^3 + 42x^2 + 208x = 2x(x^2 + 21x + 104)$$Since $b > 0$, the factor $(x + b)$ cannot come from $x$. Therefore, $(x + b)$ must be a factor of the quadratic $x^2 + 21x + 104$.

steps.title: Find the roots of the quadratic to determine the possible values of $b$
steps.content:
To find the factors of $x^2 + 21x + 104$, I'll find its roots using the quadratic formula.

The discriminant is $21^2 - 4(104) = 441 - 416 = 25$, so:$$x = \frac{-21 \pm 5}{2}$$This gives roots $x = -8$ and $x = -13$, so:$$x^2 + 21x + 104 = (x + 8)(x + 13)$$
The factors of the form $(x + b)$ with positive $b$ are $(x + 8)$ and $(x + 13)$, which means $b = 8$ or $b = 13$.

The smallest possible value is $b = 8$.
